steps.title: The Scholar's Mate
steps.text: The most famous trap: bring the queen and bishop out early to attack f7. If Black doesn't defend carefully, checkmate comes in four moves. After 1.e4 e5 2.Bc4 Nc6 3.Qh5 — if Black plays a natural-looking move like 3...Nf6?? — the game can end with 4.Qxf7#.

steps.title: The Punishment
steps.text: Against a prepared opponent, this is easily met by 3...g6 or 3...Qe7, defending f7. Knowing the trap lets you both avoid it as Black and punish careless play as White.

steps.title: Develop Don't Dash
steps.text: Most 'traps' rely on the opponent breaking basic principles — leaving the king in the center, walking into a fork, or weakening a key square like f2/f7. Develop calmly and watch for tactics every single move.
